retrieved_docs.title: Secondary Hypertension -- Etiology -- Endocrine Disorders
retrieved_docs.content: Hypertension due to endocrine disorders is caused by increased secretion of hormones. The common endocrine disorders responsible for secondary hypertension include primary aldosteronism, pheochromocytoma, and Cushing`s syndrome, and primary aldosteronism is responsible for the majority of endocrine hypertension cases. [13] Acromegaly, primary hyperparathyroidism, and thyroid disorders are very rare causes of secondary hypertension. [14]

retrieved_docs.title: Type 2 Diabetes -- Etiology -- Secondary Diabetes
retrieved_docs.content: Secondary diabetes is caused due to the complication of other diseases affecting the pancreas (for example, pancreatitis), hormone disturbances (for example, Cushing disease), or drugs (for example, corticosteroids).

retrieved_docs.title: Primary Hyperparathyroidism -- Etiology -- Genetic Conditions Associated with Primary Hyperparathyroidism
retrieved_docs.content: Up to 10% of primary hyperparathyroidism cases have a genetic basis. Genetic mutations associated with hyperparathyroidism include multiple endocrine neoplasia types 1, 2A, and 4, hyperparathyroidism-jaw tumor syndrome, familial isolated hyperparathyroidism, neonatal severe hyperparathyroidism, and familial hypocalciuric hypercalcemia. [10] [31] [32] [33] [34] A familial syndrome should be considered when primary hyperparathyroidism is diagnosed at an early age or there is a family history of hypercalcemia, pituitary adenomas, pancreatic islet cell tumors, pheochromocytomas, or medullary thyroid cancer. [35] Genetic conditions underlying primary hyperparathyroidism include:

retrieved_docs.title: Secondary Causes of Obesity and Comprehensive Diagnostic Evaluation -- Function -- Secondary Endocrine Causes of Obesity
retrieved_docs.content: Evaluating secondary causes of obesity is essential for accurate diagnosis and effective treatment planning, especially in patients who do not respond to standard lifestyle interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: Secondary Osteoporosis -- Etiology -- Endocrine Problems
retrieved_docs.content: Diabetes mellitus : Patients with type 1 diabetes have increased fracture risk. The review of the literature suggests that there is a 3-fold increased risk for any fracture and a 7-fold increased risk for hip fracture in patients with type 1 diabetes as compared to the general population. Patients with type 2 diabetes have a higher risk than the general population, with a relative risk of 1.17-2.03 in anterior sites. Although this risk is less than in patients with type 1 diabetes, the higher prevalence of type 2 diabetes makes it an important secondary cause of osteoporosis. Certain medications for diabetes, including thiazolidinediones and SGLT-2 inhibitors, have shown an increased risk in studies for osteoporosis. [8] [9] [10]

retrieved_docs.title: Secondary Osteoporosis -- Pathophysiology -- Endocrine Disorders
retrieved_docs.content: Diabetes mellitus : Excessive bone fragility in T1DM results from various factors, including defective attainment of peak bone mass, as a result of insulin deficiency or decreased IGF-I. Hypercalciuria occurring as a result of hyperglycemia can cause impaired bone mineralization. Hyperglycemia also leads to excessive accumulation of advanced glycation end products, which can accumulate in osteoblasts and impair their function. Growth factors and cytokines released from adipocytes can adversely affect bone remodeling commonly seen in obesity and insulin resistance. [37]

retrieved_docs.title: Acromegaly -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Other causes of primary GH excess include pituitary adenomas that secrete multiple hormones and GH-cell carcinomas. Important familial syndromes associated with acromegaly include Multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1, familial acromegaly, McCune-Albright syndrome, and Carney complex. GH excess can also be ectopic and produced by other tumors such as lymphoma and pancreatic-islet cell tumors. GH excess can also be iatrogenic, resulting from excessive GH administration.

retrieved_docs.title: Multiple Endocrine Neoplasias Type 4 -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Multiple endocrine neoplasia (MEN) constitutes a group of autosomal dominant disorders characterized by a broad spectrum of endocrine and nonendocrine diseases. Depending on the clinical presentations and genetic mutation, MEN is divided into different types. The most common syndrome is MEN type 1 (MEN1), characterized by primary hyperparathyroidism secondary to parathyroid gland hyperplasia, pituitary adenoma, and pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Kearns-Sayre Syndrome -- History and Physical -- Endocrine Disorders
retrieved_docs.content: Endocrine dysfunction can be the initial presenting symptom preceding other neurological manifestations of KSS. The frequency of endocrine disturbances has been reported to range from 35% to 67%, including the following: diabetes mellitus, short stature and growth hormone insufficiency, hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, adrenal insufficiency, and primary hypoparathyroidism. Therefore, endocrinologists should be aware of possible multiple-endocrine complications and regularly screen for them, given that these are potentially treatable aspects of this disease.

retrieved_docs.title: Addison Disease -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Several complications can occur due to Addison disease or secondary to inappropriate treatment. Addison disease can progress to an adrenal crisis if not recognized and promptly treated, resulting in hypotension, shock, hypoglycemia, acute cardiovascular decompensation, and death. Patients with adrenal insufficiency are also at higher risk of death due to infections, cancer, and cardiovascular causes. Delayed recognition and treatment of hypoglycemia can cause serious consequences. [42] A supraphysiologic glucocorticoid replacement can lead to the development of Cushing syndrome. [39] Growth suppression can occur in children. Premature ovarian failure or primary ovarian insufficiency may develop in up to 10% of women with Addison disease. [43]

retrieved_docs.title: Secondary Amenorrhea -- Etiology -- Other Hormonal and Endocrine Causes of Secondary Amenorrhea
retrieved_docs.content: In addition to direct abnormalities in the hypothalamus or pituitary gland, numerous other hormonal abnormalities may interact with and affect HPO axis function and result in abnormal menstruation or secondary amenorrhea, including: Hyperprolactinemia due to other causes, including lactation, stress, exercise, medications, especially antipsychotics and opiates, and chronic kidney disease Hyperandrogenism, which is seen in polycystic ovarian syndrome, exogenous androgen use (eg, as part of gender-affirming therapy), and, less commonly, in non-classical congenital adrenal hyperplasia and with androgen-secreting tumors in the adrenal gland or ovary Thyroid disorders (both hyperthyroidism and hypothyroidism) Uncontrolled diabetes Cushing syndrome Other hormonally active tumors (eg, ovarian and thyroid masses) [3] [4] [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Kabuki Syndrome -- History and Physical -- Endocrinologic
retrieved_docs.content: Individuals with this condition may also present with other abnormalities of the endocrine system, such as precocious puberty, delayed sexual development, diabetes insipidus, diabetes mellitus type II, adrenal insufficiency, hypothyroidism, and primary ovarian dysfunction. [1] [3] [5] Isolated premature thelarche is seen in up to 40% of girls with KS and is not indicative of premature puberty as it usually resolves with time. [5]

retrieved_docs.title: Hypertensive Crisis -- Etiology -- Endocrine disorders
retrieved_docs.content: Pheochromocytoma Cushing disease Primary hyperaldosteronism Renin-secreting tumors

retrieved_docs.title: Secondary Causes of Obesity and Comprehensive Diagnostic Evaluation -- Function -- Thyroid dysfunction
retrieved_docs.content: Thyroid disorders represent the most common secondary endocrine cause of weight gain, with hypothyroidism affecting up to 15% of obese patients (see Table 1 ). The European Society of Endocrinology recommends universal thyroid-stimulating hormone screening in obesity due to the high prevalence and therapeutic implications. [1] Clinical presentation includes fatigue, cold intolerance, constipation, and depression, though symptoms may be subtle in mild hypothyroidism. TSH, thyroid-stimulating hormone

retrieved_docs.title: Secondary Osteoporosis -- Etiology -- Endocrine Problems
retrieved_docs.content: Primary hyperparathyroidism (PHPT) : Generally presents in individuals over 50. It has a high prevalence of 233 cases per 100,000 women and 85 cases per 100,000 men. PHPT can result from a single gland adenoma, parathyroid gland hyperplasia, double adenoma, or rarely parathyroid carcinoma. There is also familial PHPT condition in the setting of MEN 1, MEN 4, MEN 2A and hereditary hyperparathyroidism like in jaw tumor syndrome or nonsyndromic familial isolated PHPT. [3] Cushing syndrome : Patients with Cushing syndrome have a higher risk for osteoporosis-related fractures. Bone resorption is increased, and there is decreased bone synthesis. [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Hypothyroidism -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Hypothyroidism is primarily categorized as primary and secondary (ie, central) hypothyroidism. In primary hypothyroidism, the thyroid gland cannot produce adequate amounts of thyroid hormone. The less commonly seen secondary or central hypothyroidism occurs when the thyroid gland functions normally; however, hypothyroidism results from the abnormal pituitary gland or hypothalamus function.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 2 -- Community 1267
retrieved_docs.content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES is a broad category that includes several specific pancreatic endocrine disorders. Among its children are ارتفاع السكر في الدم, which describes the elevated blood‑glucose levels that characterize hyperglycemia; مرض السكري دون مضاعفات, which refers to diabetes that has not yet produced secondary complications; فرط أنسولين الدم, indicating an excess of circulating insulin often seen in early insulin‑resistance states or insulin‑producing tumors; and ورم غلوكاغوني, a rare glucagon‑secreting tumor (glucagonoma) that can also cause hyperglycemia. These conditions are interrelated through their impact on pancreatic hormone production and regulation of blood glucose, highlighting the importance of comprehensive assessment and management of pancreatic endocrine function in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2175
retrieved_docs.content: Triiodothyronine (trijodthyronin) is a thyroid hormone that is synthesized and secreted by the thyroid gland (štítná žláza). The štítná žláza is also the primary anatomical site where thyroid insufficiency (قصور الدَّرَقِيَّة), a condition characterized by reduced production of thyroid hormones, is identified. Recognizing the relationship between trijodthyronin and the štítná žláza, as well as the gland’s role in قصور الدَّرَقِيَّة, is essential for accurate diagnosis and effective management of disorders affecting thyroid hormone metabolism.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 1 -- Community 194
retrieved_docs.content: The classification “DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES” is situated within the broader category of “other endocrine disorders.” Within this group, several specific conditions are identified as subtypes or manifestations: “ارتفاع السكر في الدم” (hyperglycemia) represents elevated blood glucose levels; “مرض السكري دون مضاعفات” denotes diabetes mellitus without complications; “نقص تركيز السكر” (hypoglycemia) reflects abnormally low blood glucose and is also recognized as a component of the wider “other endocrine disorders” category; “فرط أنسولين الدم” (hyperinsulinemia) describes excessive circulating insulin; “ورم غلوكاغوني” (glucagonoma) is a pancreatic tumor that secretes glucagon; and “اضطراب إفراز الجسترين” (gastrin secretion disorder) indicates abnormal gastrin release. The gastrin secretion disorder further leads to “الحثل وإضطرابات الإفراز في الغشاء المخاطي المَعِدِيّ المِعَوِيّ” (gastric mucosal atrophy and associated secretion abnormalities), illustrating a downstream effect on the gastric mucosa. Together, these relationships delineate how diabetes‑related endocrine pancreatic diseases encompass a spectrum of hyper‑ and hypoglycemic states, hormone‑excess conditions, neoplastic processes, and secondary gastrointestinal disturbances, all nested within the larger framework of endocrine disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 1756
retrieved_docs.content: The category “DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES” encompasses a spectrum of disorders and clinical manifestations that arise from dysfunction of the pancreatic endocrine system. It includes “ارتفاع السكر في الدم” (hyperglycemia), which represents elevated blood‑glucose levels and is a primary feature of diabetes. “غيبوبة فرط الأسمولية السكري” (diabetic hyperosmolar coma) is a severe, acute complication of uncontrolled hyperglycemia characterized by extreme dehydration and altered mental status. “مرض السكري دون مضاعفات” (diabetes mellitus without complications) denotes diabetes in its uncomplicated form, serving as the baseline condition from which other sequelae may develop. Among the chronic complications, “اعتلال عصبي سكري” (diabetic neuropathy) reflects nerve damage caused by prolonged hyperglycemia, leading to sensory and motor deficits. “ضمور نصف الوجه المترقي” (progressive hemifacial atrophy) is listed as a possible extra‑pancreatic manifestation that can be associated with underlying endocrine pancreatic disease. Metabolic dysregulation is further illustrated by “فرط أنسولين الدم” (hyperinsulinemia), an excess of circulating insulin that may precede or accompany insulin resistance. Finally, “ورم غلوكاغوني” (glucagonoma) represents a rare pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor that secretes excess glucagon, producing a distinct clinical syndrome. Together, these entities illustrate the diverse acute, chronic, and neoplastic conditions linked to diseases of the endocrine pancreas, highlighting the importance of comprehensive assessment and management of both glycemic control and associated complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 3 -- Community 2517
retrieved_docs.content: Cancer of thyroid is classified as a type of اضطراب صماوي, indicating that it belongs to the broader category of endocrine disorders. By recognizing thyroid cancer as an endocrine disorder, clinicians understand that the disease originates in the hormone‑producing thyroid gland and shares pathophysiological features with other conditions affecting the endocrine system. This classification guides diagnostic evaluation, treatment planning, and prognostic assessment within the context of endocrine pathology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 1930
retrieved_docs.content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES is a broad classification that encompasses several distinct conditions. Within this category, مرض السكري دون مضاعفات refers to diabetes without complications, representing a form of the disease where hyperglycemia is present but secondary complications have not yet developed. فرط أنسولين الدم denotes hyperinsulinemia, a state of elevated insulin levels in the blood that can arise from pancreatic beta‑cell overactivity or insulin‑secreting tumors and may lead to hypoglycemia. ورم غلوكاغوني describes glucagonoma, a rare pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor that secretes excess glucagon, causing characteristic metabolic disturbances such as glucose intolerance, weight loss, and a distinctive skin rash. Together, these three entities illustrate the spectrum of disorders that fall under DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES, highlighting the varied clinical presentations and management considerations associated with pancreatic endocrine pathology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 4 -- Community 2721
retrieved_docs.content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES is a broad category that includes ارتفاع السكر في الدم, اعتلال عصبي سكري, فرط أنسولين الدم, and ورم غلوكاغوني. ارتفاع السكر في الدم (high blood sugar) is a primary manifestation of diabetes, reflecting impaired glucose regulation by the endocrine pancreas. اعتلال عصبي سكري (diabetic neuropathy) is a common chronic complication of prolonged hyperglycemia, affecting peripheral nerves and leading to sensory and motor deficits. فرط أنسولين الدم (hyperinsulinemia) denotes excessive insulin secretion, which can occur in early insulin resistance or insulin‑producing tumors and may precede or accompany diabetes. ورم غلوكاغوني (glucagonoma) is a rare pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor that secretes excess glucagon, often resulting in persistent hyperglycemia and weight loss. Recognizing how these conditions are interrelated within endocrine pancreatic disease supports comprehensive diagnosis, monitoring, and therapeutic strategies for affected patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 2 -- Community 584
retrieved_docs.content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES is classified as a subset of other endocrine disorders and serves as a parent category for several specific pancreatic and metabolic conditions. Within this group are ارتفاع السكر في الدم (hyperglycemia), مرض السكري دون مضاعفات (diabetes without complications), نقص تركيز السكر (hypoglycemia), اضطراب إفراز الجسترين (disorder of gastrointestinal secretion), اعتلال عصبي سكري (diabetic neuropathy), فرط أنسولين الدم (hyperinsulinemia), and ورم غلوكاغوني (glucagonoma). The condition نقص تركيز السكر (hypoglycemia) is also recognized as a child of other endocrine disorders, underscoring its broader relevance across endocrine pathology. Moreover, اضطراب إفراز الجسترين (disorder of gastrointestinal secretion) encompasses الحثل واضطرابات الإفراز في الغشاء المخاطي المَعِدِيّ المِعَوِيّ (sclerosis and secretion disorders of the gastric and intestinal mucosa), linking pancreatic endocrine dysfunction with secondary mucosal abnormalities. Together, these relationships illustrate how diseases of the endocrine pancreas, including various glucose‑regulating disorders and their complications, are integrated within the larger framework of endocrine system disorders, informing comprehensive diagnosis and management strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 4 -- Community 2841
retrieved_docs.content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES is a classification that encompasses several distinct disorders of pancreatic endocrine function. It includes مرض السكري دون مضاعفات, which denotes diabetes mellitus without any associated complications and represents the basic metabolic disturbance of chronic hyperglycemia. It also comprises اعتلال عصبي سكري, a chronic diabetic neuropathy in which prolonged hyperglycemia damages peripheral nerves, leading to sensory loss, pain, and autonomic dysfunction. The category further lists ضمور نصف الوجه المترقي, a progressive atrophy of one side of the facial tissues that can be observed in some patients with endocrine pancreatic disease. فرط أنسولين الدم is another child condition, describing an excess of circulating insulin that may arise from insulin‑producing tumors (insulinoma) or other dysregulation of pancreatic β‑cell activity. Finally, ورم غلوكاغوني refers to a glucagon‑secreting pancreatic tumor (glucagonoma) that causes hyperglycemia and a characteristic clinical syndrome. Together, these entities illustrate the full spectrum of pancreatic endocrine disorders—from uncomplicated diabetes to its neuropathic complications, hormonal excess states, and neoplastic growths—underscoring the need for comprehensive evaluation and targeted management in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 3 -- Community 2451
retrieved_docs.content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES is positioned within the larger classification of other endocrine disorders, which itself is nested under the broader endocrine condition described by نقص تركيز السكر (hypoglycemia). Within this disease group, several specific entities are identified as subcategories: مرض السكري دون مضاعفات (diabetes without complications), نقص تركيز السكر (hypoglycemia) as a distinct clinical manifestation, فرط أنسولين الدم (hyperinsulinemia), and ورم غلوكاغوني (glucagonoma). This hierarchical arrangement reflects how individual pancreatic endocrine disorders—ranging from uncomplicated diabetes to excess insulin production and glucagon‑secreting tumors—are interconnected with each other and with more general endocrine disease categories, supporting a comprehensive approach to diagnosis, assessment, and patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 1875
retrieved_docs.content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES is a broad category that includes several related disorders: ارتفاع السكر في الدم (elevated blood glucose), مرض السكري دون مضاعفات (diabetes without complications), اعتلال عصبي سكري (diabetic neuropathy), فرط أنسولين الدم (hyperinsulinemia), and ورم غلوكاغوني (glucagonoma). These entities represent a spectrum of pancreatic endocrine dysfunction, ranging from simple hyperglycemia to specific complications such as neuropathy, as well as hormonal excess states like hyperinsulinemia and glucagon‑secreting tumors. Understanding that all of these conditions are subtypes of diseases of the endocrine pancreas, including diabetes, helps clinicians recognize the varied presentations, anticipate potential complications, and tailor comprehensive patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 1970
retrieved_docs.content: The category “DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES” is positioned within the broader group of “other endocrine disorders” and encompasses a range of specific pancreatic and metabolic conditions. Among its sub‑entities are “مرض السكري دون مضاعفات” (diabetes without complications), “نقص تركيز السكر” (hypoglycemia), “اعتلال عصبي سكري” (diabetic neuropathy), “ضمور نصف الوجه المترقي” (progressive hemifacial atrophy), “فرط أنسولين الدم” (hyperinsulinemia), and “ورم غلوكاغوني” (glucagonoma). Hypoglycemia is therefore recognized both as a distinct manifestation under the diabetes‑related pancreatic disease spectrum and as a condition that belongs to the larger classification of other endocrine disorders. Collectively, these entities illustrate the diverse clinical presentations that can arise from endocrine dysfunction of the pancreas, highlighting the importance of recognizing both isolated metabolic disturbances and their broader endocrine context for accurate diagnosis and comprehensive patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1545
retrieved_docs.content: The category “DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES” encompasses a spectrum of pancreatic and metabolic disorders. It includes “مرض السكري دون مضاعفات” (diabetes mellitus without complications) as the baseline form of the disease, as well as “مرض السكري نوع 2” (type 2 diabetes), which represents the most common adult‑onset variant characterized by insulin resistance. Severe acute metabolic decompensation can manifest as “غيبوبة فرط الأسمولية السكري” (diabetic hyperosmolar coma), a life‑threatening state of extreme hyperglycemia and dehydration. Chronic complications of diabetes are reflected in “اعتلال عصبي سكري” (diabetic neuropathy), a progressive nerve damage that can cause pain, sensory loss, and autonomic dysfunction. Hormonal dysregulation associated with pancreatic endocrine tumors is represented by “فرط أنسولين الدم” (hyperinsulinemia), often due to insulin‑secreting neoplasms, and “ورم غلوكاغوني” (glucagonoma), a rare tumor that overproduces glucagon leading to characteristic skin and metabolic findings. Additionally, the list includes “ضمور نصف الوجه المترقي” (progressive facial hemi‑atrophy), a condition that, while not a classic diabetic complication, may be observed in the broader context of endocrine pancreatic disease. Together, these entities illustrate the diverse clinical presentations—from uncomplicated hyperglycemia to acute metabolic crises, chronic neuropathic damage, and endocrine neoplasms—associated with diseases of the endocrine pancreas, underscoring the importance of comprehensive evaluation and management in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 4 -- Community 2750
retrieved_docs.content: Other endocrine disorders serve as a broader category that includes the specific condition known as carbohydrate metabolism disorder (اضطراب استقلاب الكربوهيدرات), indicating that this metabolic disorder is classified under the umbrella of other endocrine disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 2046
retrieved_docs.content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES encompasses a spectrum of related conditions. It includes ارتفاع السكر في الدم, which denotes elevated blood glucose levels, and مرض السكري دون مضاعفات, representing diabetes without complications. Neurological involvement is reflected by اعتلال عصبي سكري, a diabetic neuropathy. Facial manifestations may appear as ضمور نصف الوجه المترقي, a progressive hemi‑facial atrophy that can be associated with pancreatic endocrine disorders. Metabolic dysregulation is further illustrated by فرط أنسولين الدم, an excess of circulating insulin, and by ورم غلوكاغوني, a glucagon‑producing tumor of the pancreas. Together these entities illustrate the diverse clinical presentations and complications that arise from diseases of the endocrine pancreas, highlighting the importance of comprehensive assessment and management in affected patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 3 -- Community 1766
retrieved_docs.content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES is classified as a subgroup of other endocrine disorders. Within this pancreatic endocrine disease category are several specific conditions: ارتفاع السكر في الدم (hyperglycemia or elevated blood glucose), نقص تركيز السكر (hypoglycemia or low blood glucose), اعتلال عصبي سكري (diabetic neuropathy), فرط أنسولين الدم (hyperinsulinemia or excess insulin in the blood), and ورم غلوكاغوني (glucagonoma, a tumor that secretes excess glucagon). These entities together represent the spectrum of metabolic disturbances, chronic complications, and neoplastic processes that arise from dysfunction of the pancreatic endocrine cells. Hyperglycemia and hypoglycemia illustrate opposite dysregulations of glucose homeostasis, while hyperinsulinemia reflects overproduction of insulin and glucagonoma reflects overproduction of glucagon. Diabetic neuropathy is a long‑term complication that develops from persistent hyperglycemia. All of these conditions are linked under the broader umbrella of other endocrine disorders, highlighting their common basis in hormone imbalance. Although one relationship suggested that نقص تركيز السكر might be a parent of other endocrine disorders, the more coherent hierarchy places DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES as the child of other endocrine disorders, with نقص تركيز السكر correctly understood as one of its constituent manifestations.
